facts.subject: Article 18 of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999
facts.object: the difference between quotations or prices on the world market for the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of that Regulation and prices for those products within the Community may be covered by an export refund
facts.natural_language: Article 18 of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 provides that the difference between quotations or prices on the world market for the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of that Regulation and prices for those products within the Community may be covered by an export refund.

source_locator.quote: (1) Article 18 of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 provides that the difference between quotations or prices on the world market for the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of that Regulation and prices for those products within the Community may be covered by an export refund.

extra.model_quote: (1) Article 18 of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 provides that the difference between quotations or prices on the world market for the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of that Regulation and prices for those products within the Community may be covered by an export refund.

facts.subject: Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999
facts.object: when refunds on white and raw sugar, undenatured and exported in its unaltered state, are being fixed account must be taken of the situation on the Community and world markets in sugar and in particular of the price and cost factors set out in Article 19 of that Regulation
facts.natural_language: Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 stipulates that when fixing refunds for undenatured white and raw sugar exported in its unaltered state, account must be taken of the situation on Community and world sugar markets, specifically price and cost factors from Article 19.

source_locator.quote: Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 provides that when refunds on white and raw sugar, undenatured and exported in its unaltered state, are being fixed account must be taken of the situation on the Community and world markets in sugar and in particular of the price and cost factors set out in Article 19 of that Regulation

extra.model_quote: Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999 provides that when refunds on white and raw sugar, undenatured and exported in its unaltered state, are being fixed account must be taken of the situation on the Community and world markets in sugar and in particular of the price and cost factors set out in Article 19 of that Regulation

facts.object: the economic aspect of the proposed exports should also be taken into account

facts.subject: the refund thus calculated for sugar containing added flavouring or colouring matter
facts.natural_language: The refund thus calculated for sugar containing added flavouring or colouring matter must apply to their sucrose content.

facts.subject: the refund thus calculated for sugar containing added flavouring or colouring matter
facts.natural_language: The refund thus calculated for sugar containing added flavouring or colouring matter must be fixed per 1 % of the said content.

facts.subject: the export refund
facts.natural_language: The export refund must be fixed every two weeks.

facts.subject: Commission Regulation (EC) No 1156/2001
facts.object: the export refunds on the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999, undenatured and exported in the natural state
facts.natural_language: The Commission fixes the export refunds for undenatured white and raw sugar exported in their natural state to the amounts listed in the Annex.

source_locator.quote: The export refunds on the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999, undenatured and exported in the natural state, are hereby fixed to the amounts shown in the Annex hereto.

extra.model_quote: The export refunds on the products listed in Article 1(1)(a) of Regulation (EC) No 2038/1999, undenatured and exported in the natural state, are hereby fixed to the amounts shown in the Annex hereto.

facts.subject: Commission Regulation (EC) No 1156/2001
facts.natural_language: Commission Regulation (EC) No 1156/2001 enters into force on 14 June 2001.

facts.subject: Commission Regulation (EC) No 1156/2001
facts.predicate: is binding in its entirety and directly applicable in
facts.natural_language: Commission Regulation (EC) No 1156/2001 is binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.
